tweets.full_text: AI開発の本質は速度ではなく「共感の再現」にある。Claude Codeの30分解説は単なる手法紹介を超え、vibe-codingで如何にAIが人間の感性を理解し再現するかを示す教科書だ。AI時代に求められるのはコードの羅列ではなく「感情の翻訳者」としての技術だと改めて気づかされる。これが次世代AIの真骨頂である。

tweets.full_text:
最近のAIコーディング周り、これだけ動きがある。

①OpenAIが「Codex Sites」を発表。企画書やダッシュボード、アイデアを渡すだけでURLつきのインタラクティブなアプリが生成される。ノーコード→"プロンプトだけ"の流れがまた一段進んだ形。

②Claude Platformにターミナル機能が追加。API呼び出し、エージェント起動、ファイルアップロード、YAML同期、実行ログの確認までCLI一つで完結。Claude Codeからも利用可能で、開発者向けの実用度が一気に上がった。

③Uberがコーディングエージェントの利用上限を「従業員1人あたり月$1,500/ツール」に設定したとの報道。裏を返せば、1人月$1,500分の価値があるとUber自身が認めているということでもある。

プラットフォーム側は機能拡張を加速し、導入企業側はコスト管理フェーズに入りつつある。AIコーディングツールが"試す段階"から"運用の仕組みを整える段階"に移行している兆候が揃ってきた。

user.name: Zane Chen
user.description: Open source. Dev Tool. AI. Web Tool. The stuff worth your time.

tweets.full_text:
Its nearly impossible to start a successful product with $0 for marketing, you simply can't

I can tell you that you should set aside at least $1-2k at least for various bits. Like:

- listing in directories
- acquiring a few backlinks for seo
- getting claude code sub for seo articles
- getting ahrefs subscription for seo
- listing in paid directories
- paying for various subscriptions

I would say if you really want results, you need to invest at least 3k per month for a few months.

Otherwise you will end up working in with no perspective or seeing the needle move for months on end (even 12 months)

Dont tell me just start another app if the first one doesnt work, and do the same until one works

I can bet you 99% of the indie hackers do the same, just launch apps hoping one will stick...

Set aside $10k for marketing, and make sure your idea and your app really solves a problem for your customer

You will 100% win in 6-12 months making that 10k to be 100x.

I am not just saying that because I offer seo services, automation services or anything like that

I am telling you the reality

tweets.full_text:
Sources for the above:

Active incident confirmed — https://t.co/ufEY7h7yqX (June 5, "Elevated errors on many Claude models" across Claude API, Claude Code, https://t.co/fghZcOGKfm, Claude Cowork): https://t.co/XBy2vjoz8s

Cross-tenant leak claim — @kimmonismus on X, single source, unverified, confidence LOW (~30%): https://t.co/JCD7yhH5M2

Prior postmortem confirming Anthropic's post-incident transparency pattern — Anthropic Engineering Blog, April 23, 2026: https://t.co/iHFOJiuaxI

No CVE. No adversary. Accidental disclosure scenario — watch for Anthropic's post-incident report for confirmation or denial of cross-tenant routing.

Cross-tenant inference leak during an outage. If confirmed, this is not a routine availability incident — it's a confidentiality breach.

Anthropic's status page confirms a live multi-service outage today, June 5: "Elevated errors on many Claude models" is the active incident, affecting Claude API, Claude Code, https://t.co/fghZcOGKfm, and Claude Cowork. That part is verified. What is not yet confirmed is the claim surfacing on X from @kimmonismus — that at least one user received another user's inference output during the disruption. Anthropic has not confirmed a data breach.

The claim carries low confidence. A single account, no corroborating screenshots, no independent confirmation from other users or researchers. Signal-worthy, not fact. Treat it as such.

The failure mode is technically plausible, though. During high-load routing failures, response queues can mis-sequence or mis-route at the infrastructure layer — crossed wires at the load balancer or caching layer, not necessarily a software vulnerability, potentially a race condition under stress. This same class of failure appeared briefly in OpenAI's ChatGPT in March 2023, when users saw fragments of other users' conversation titles. It always does, eventually.

What separates this from a standard outage if it holds: availability incidents are inconvenient. Confidentiality breaches carry actual legal weight. Under GDPR Article 33 framing and most enterprise API agreements, a scenario where User A receives User B's inference output constitutes unauthorized disclosure — potentially of sensitive personal or business data. That triggers breach notification obligations for enterprise customers in the EU, UK, and several US states. The distinction between "the API was slow" and "the API gave you someone else's data" is not subtle.

The status page history also shows this is Anthropic's third significant incident in four days — June 2 (widespread outage), June 3 (elevated errors on Opus 4.7), and now June 5. Three incidents in four days is a reliability pattern, not a run of bad luck. Enterprise procurement teams notice patterns. The reputational math compounds faster than the technical one.

If your team is running Claude API calls in production today, treat any responses from this outage window as potentially unreliable — not just for accuracy but for data provenance. If you're processing sensitive user data through the API, a precautionary audit of responses returned during the outage window is worth the hour before the next sprint.

The dispositive document will be Anthropic's post-incident report. Their April 23, 2026 Claude Code quality postmortem shows they publish detailed technical write-ups when things go wrong — so one should be expected here. Watch for whether it specifically addresses cross-tenant data routing. That's the answer.

Cross-tenant claim: unverified. Outage: confirmed. The distinction matters — until it doesn't.

Anthropic building superhuman coder with nation-state hacking capabilities

Clark told new hires to get hobbies that are not computers because they will not need so many coders

> Claude Code already made “software engineer” feel less permanent
> Anthropic is openly warning about AI building its own successors
> Jack Clark said frontier models are moving into cyber and national security territory
> Claude Gov is already built for classified government work
> now the company is telling recruits not to make computers their whole life

should we be worried about that?

tweets.full_text:
Claude Code inventor Boris Cherny: why inexperience is now the most valuable asset in tech

"If you look at the number of startups today or like maybe in the next, you know, the past 10 years, I think the number of startups in the next 10 years that are just going to like disrupt everything is going to increase like 10x.

Because right now you can be a tiny startup, you could build a thing that's as valuable as a large company, and you can actually compete head to head.

Because the large company has to evolve their business process, they have to evolve the way they work, they have to retrain everyone to use technology, they're going to face a lot of internal resistance to that.

But, you know, no one here has that problem. If you're starting fresh, then you can kind of build with AI natively from the ground up.

So I don't know. I think it's the best time to build. It's the best time to be a startup. There's so much disruption coming."

— @bcherny of @AnthropicAI interviewed by @sequoia partner @laurenmhreeder at AI Ascent 2026

tweets.full_text:
Así escala de verdad un Senior Engineer con Claude Code.

La diferencia está en mover tu tiempo hacia lo que más importa:
→ mejores prompts, más planificación, más review, menos tecleo.

El workflow:
usa un plugin que divide cada tarea entre 5 agentes:
- uno hace brainstorming
- otro diseña el plan técnico
- otro implementa
- otro revisa
- otro valida distintos ángulos del proyecto

Todo queda documentado en markdown. 

Es más lento, hay más espera, pero la calidad sube porque cada agente tiene un rol claro.

Y luego viene el multiplicador real:
→ git worktrees

Si Claude Code ya te hace más rápido, los worktrees te dejan lanzar varias sesiones en paralelo, cada una trabajando en una tarea distinta.

Su equipo corre 4–8 sesiones de Claude Code a la vez.

La nueva skill de ingeniería no es solo saber codear.

Es saber dirigir varios agentes sin perder el control del sistema.

tweets.full_text:
Anthropic'in geçen hafta sessiz sedasız yayınladığı bir repo var, çoğu kişi fark etmedi bile.

`defending-code-reference-harness` deniyor. Özünde ne var; Claude Code ile çalışan bir güvenlik açığı bulma pipeline'ı. Ama asıl ilginç kısım şu, repoya birlikte gelen skill'ler gerçekten kullanılabilir düzeyde.

Şöyle çalışıyor. Repoyu klonlayıp Claude Code ile açıyorsun, `/quickstart` ile başlıyorsun. Oradan `/threat-model` komutuyla önce ne arayacağını modele söylüyorsun, kendi codebase'in için bir tehdit modeli çıkarıyor. Sonra `/vuln-scan` ile static tarama, `/triage` ile hangilerinin gerçek sorun olduğuna bakıyorsun, `/patch` ile de düzeltme önerisi alıyorsun. Beş ayrı adım, beş ayrı skill.

Benim en çok dikkatimi çeken şey şu oldu. Harness kısmı C/C++ memory vulnerability'lerine odaklanmış, ama `/customize` komutu var ve orada "bunu Java'ya nasıl taşırım" diye sorabiliyorsun. Yani template mantığı var.

OSINT açısından bakarsak, tehdit modelleme adımı aslında kaynak doğrulama mantığıyla birebir örtüşüyor. Neye bakacağını tanımlamadan taramaya başlamak nasıl anlamsızsa burada da aynı şey var.

Küçük ama nadir bir repo bu. Açık kaynak, Anthropic'in kendi güvenlik yaklaşımının referans implementasyonu olarak tanımlanmış.

https://t.co/iKKEtACaVA

quoted_status.full_text:
作为一个老 Codex 用户，经常烦恼 Codex 过度谨慎。

于是我专门开源了个 skill 叫格局（geju），专治 Codex  稳稳接住“苟帝”综合征

- 经常因为害怕破坏东西，保留旧行为、旧命名、旧路径、别名、shim 和双轨流程。
- 经常在看清整个系统前，就钻进一个字段、一个函数、一个段落或一条迁移路径。
- 经常给出礼貌、平衡、低风险但不解决问题的答案。

安装完这个 skill 之后，使用「格局打开」这个关键词就可以把 codex 秒变暴论输出机。

内置「从终局倒推」/「零历史包袱假设」/「反向约束」等方法论，对抗四大求稳问题。

你甚至能从 codex 身上找到一点 claude 4.6 的味道。

不承诺回答百分百正确，但求答案对你有极强的启发性。

让 codex 格局打开！

https://t.co/EqXeG6dxsq
